concepts.site_goal: Serve as a curated editorial reference database for gem collectors, jewelry designers, and gemology students, providing authoritative classification, grading, and visual reference across all major gem species
concepts.description: A jewel-toned dark-luxury editorial reference site presenting gemological knowledge as a collector's archive. Deep navy and charcoal page backgrounds contrast with saturated gemstone accent colors — emerald, sapphire, ruby, amethyst, citrine — applied per gem family. Classification cards fill dense grid layouts, each card subtly keyed to its gem's color family. Navigation follows a curated collection model with a persistent side rail and breadcrumb trails through eight detailed reference sections.
concepts.motif: jewel-toned dark luxury editorial — deep navy and charcoal backgrounds (#1c1c2e base) with saturated gemstone accent colors shifting by gem family, gold rule dividers, serif specimen labels, and editorial pull-quote typography

pages.layout_pattern: full-width hero with featured specimen spotlight + horizontal curated card strip + editorial gem-of-the-week panel
sections: hero featured specimen with large photograph and classification badge
gem of the week editorial spotlight
quick-navigation taxonomy grid by mineral class

pages.layout_pattern: filterable 4-column classification card grid with left sidebar facet filters by mineral system, hardness, crystal structure, and gem family
sections: filter sidebar with collapsible facet groups

pages.layout_pattern: tabbed reference panels per gem type with full-width comparison charts, visual grading scales, and certification equivalency matrix
sections: clarity grading chart with inclusion diagrams

pages.layout_pattern: masonry gallery grid with slide-in detail drawer showing SVG facet diagrams, proportions, and recommended gem types
sections: masonry gallery grid of cut illustrations
facet count and angle reference table
historical development and regional origin notes

pages.layout_pattern: full-bleed interactive SVG world map with clickable deposit markers and collapsible regional detail panel on the right
sections: world map canvas with deposit point markers
deposit type and gem family legend
regional detail panel with mine profiles

pages.layout_pattern: alphabetical sticky index rail on left with scrollable definition entries, inline cross-reference links, and search input at top
sections: definition entry list with gem family tags

pages.layout_pattern: structured guide pages with multi-column lab comparison table, lab profile cards, and treatment disclosure reference
sections: certification overview and why it matters
major lab comparison table (GIA, AGL, Gübelin, SSEF)
how to read a grading report guide
treatment disclosure standards by gem type

pages.layout_pattern: editorial long-form single-column with pull quotes, contributor bios, and sourcing methodology notes

message_intent: establish authoritative expertise in gemological reference
make complex classification data visually accessible and browsable
evoke the luxury and rarity of fine gemstones through UI aesthetic
support both casual collector browsing and deep gemologist research
content_model: GemSpecimen: name, species, variety, trade names, hardness, crystal system, refractive index, specific gravity, treatment status, origin regions, images
CutStyle: name, category, facet count, crown angle, pavilion angle, SVG diagram, best gem types, historical notes
GradingScale: gem type, grading authority, color descriptor scale, clarity grade map, cut proportion ranges
GlossaryTerm: term, definition, etymology, gem families, related term links
OriginDeposit: country, region, coordinates, gem types, geological period, deposit type, notable mines
interactions: facet filter selection and reset on mineral index sidebar
gem type tab switching on grading page
deposit marker click revealing regional detail panel on origins map
cut gallery masonry card opening detail drawer
hover jewel-tone color-shift on gem classification cards

animations.description: On mouseenter, the gem classification card's CSS background-color transitions from deep charcoal (#1c1c2e) to a rich jewel tone keyed to the card's data-gem-family attribute — #0d3d2e for beryl, #0e1f4a for blue corundum, #3d0e1a for red corundum, #2a0e4a for amethyst, #3d2e00 for chrysoberyl — while border-color transitions from #3a3a4a to the gem's saturated accent (#2ecc71, #3498db, #e74c3c, #9b59b6, #f1c40f respectively); timing is ease-in-out over 600ms; on mouseleave all colors return to base values over the same duration; card position, size, and page layout remain entirely unchanged throughout

asset_needs: SVG world map of major gem deposit locations with coordinate data
SVG facet diagram illustrations for 20+ cut styles
high-resolution mineral specimen photographs per gem species
grading scale gradient graphics for color and clarity
gem family color palette design tokens
concepts.mobile_behavior: single-column stacked layout; bottom-sheet filter drawer replaces sidebar; origins map switches to searchable regional list; masonry gallery becomes 2-column

concepts.site_goal: Provide professional gemologists, researchers, and advanced collectors with a rigorous, data-dense reference covering mineral taxonomy, multi-lab certification comparison, cutting geometry, and geological provenance in a warm archival museum-catalog aesthetic
concepts.description: A warm-dark museum-archive reference database styled as a professional gemologist's research vault. Obsidian and deep walnut-brown backgrounds with burnished gold rule lines and monospaced data fields evoke a museum catalog or scientific ledger. Content is organized taxonomically — mineral class → species → variety → trade name — with dense comparison tables, precision SVG cut diagrams, and cross-referenced glossary entries. Navigation uses a persistent hierarchical taxonomy tree sidebar. The site serves the professional tier: accuracy, citation depth, and cross-referencing workflows take precedence over decorative presentation.
concepts.motif: museum archive vault — warm obsidian (#181510) and deep walnut-brown backgrounds, burnished gold (#c9a84c) rule dividers and accent text, monospaced data fields for measurements, serif taxonomy labels, archival document-style section headers

pages.layout_pattern: editorial landing with large featured mineral photograph on left, three-column highlights grid on right, and persistent taxonomy tree sidebar
sections: featured mineral of the week with full-bleed photograph
full-text search bar with field selectors

pages.layout_pattern: hierarchical taxonomy tree on left with data-dense specimen record cards in main column, sortable by mineral class, hardness, SG, or crystal system
stacked accordion reference panels per gem type, each containing visual grading scales, clarity inclusion diagrams, treatment disclosure tables, and authority comparison notes
sections: color grading gradient scale with descriptor labels
treatment type and detectability reference table

pages.layout_pattern: 3-column illustrated card grid with SVG facet diagrams; each card lifts on hover and expands inline to show cut proportions, facet count, angular data table, and gem suitability rating
sections: cut category filter tabs (brilliant, step, mixed, fancy, cabochon)
cut style card grid with SVG diagrams
inline expansion panel with proportion data
facet count and angle reference table
historical origin and regional variation notes

pages.layout_pattern: annotated world map with deposit point markers, geological period timeline range filter, gem-type overlay toggle, and slide-in regional detail panel
sections: world map with sized deposit markers
slide-in regional detail panel with mine profiles

pages.layout_pattern: search-driven full-width glossary with A-Z tab navigation, inline cross-reference chips, related gem family tags per entry, and etymology notes
sections: full-text search input with instant filtering
glossary entry list with definition, etymology, and cross-references

pages.layout_pattern: side-by-side scrollable comparison columns for major gem labs with attribute rows, trust indicators, and report format sample panels
structured two-column layout with editorial mission on left and methodology, citations, version log, and contributor roster on right

message_intent: project rigorous professional authority in gemological science
support data-dense research and cross-referencing workflows for experts
differentiate from consumer gem guides through precision, citation, and taxonomic depth
provide a trustworthy archival resource that professionals can cite in reports
content_model: MineralSpecies: IMA-recognized name, chemical formula, crystal system, hardness, SG, RI range, varieties list, type locality, references
CutStyleRecord: name, category, facet count, crown angle range, pavilion angle range, ideal L/W ratio, origin period, SVG diagram path
GradingReference: gem type, grading authority, color descriptor scale array, clarity grade map, treatment types and detectability
CertificationLab: name, abbreviation, founded year, headquarters, recognized gem types, avg turnaround, report verification URL, jurisdiction acceptance
GemDeposit: country, region, coordinates, primary gem types, geological age, deposit type, notable mine names
interactions: taxonomy tree node expand and collapse on species index
accordion panel open and close on grading scales page
cut card hover lift motion and inline expansion click on cutting styles
geological period timeline range drag on origins map
gem type overlay toggle on origins map
lab checkbox selection for side-by-side comparison table

animations.description: On mouseenter, the cut style card applies CSS transform: translateY(-6px) scale(1.04) using cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.46, 0.45, 0.94) easing over 350ms; the SVG facet diagram and all card text content are carried along as children of the same element; on mouseleave the card returns to transform: translateY(0) scale(1) over 350ms with the same easing; the card's grid cell retains its original dimensions throughout via a fixed height container so no sibling cards shift; no scroll, no layout reflow, no DOM replacement occurs

asset_needs: SVG facet diagrams for 30+ cut styles with labeled facet groups
annotated world map SVG with deposit coordinate dataset
grading scale gradient and inclusion diagram graphics
anonymized lab report format sample images
concepts.mobile_behavior: single-column layout; taxonomy tree collapses into top-sheet drawer; comparison tables scroll horizontally with frozen first column; origins map switches to searchable deposit list view
